title: Error: Unknown setting for --compiler: appleclang
body:
This is a bug with CMake support on macOS.

Since [CMP0025](https://cmake.org/cmake/help/v3.0/policy/CMP0025.html), Compiler id for Apple Clang is now `AppleClang` instead of `Clang`. With the following rule in `/opt/local/share/verilator/verilator-config.cmake`,

```
  string(TOLOWER ${CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID} COMPILER)
  if (NOT COMPILER MATCHES "msvc|clang")
    set(COMPILER gcc)
  endif()
```

CMake-based projects will have errors like
```
%Error: Unknown setting for --compiler: appleclang
```

since `${CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID}` is `AppleClang` on macOS, and thus `${COMPILER}` will be set to `appleclang`, a value not accepted by verilator.
code_blocks:
string(TOLOWER ${CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID} COMPILER)
  if (NOT COMPILER MATCHES "msvc|clang")
    set(COMPILER gcc)
  endif()
